title: codiaeum oligogynum
intro: Codiaeum oligogynum, also known as the 'butterfly ginger', is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its bright orange-red bracts and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, with medicinal properties attributed to the rhizome, which is said to possess analgesic and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Codiaeum oligogynum include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
